titleStmt.title: SII 18.52: original edition by N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 52. (B.K. No. 9 of 1932-33.) BIDARGAḌḌI, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.hi: Broken slab near the temple of Basavaṇṇa.

summary.p: This broken record refers itself to the reign of Jagadēkamalla Jaya[siṁhadēva]. It states that Kundarāja, son (maga) of Iṛivabeḍaṅgadēva was governing Banavāsi Twelve-thousand and his wife Paṁpādēvi was administering Jiḍugūr. Kaṇvanṛipa of the Sēnamalla family and manneya of Bāsavūr Hundred-and-forty is mentioned.
The record being fragmentary, its purport cannot be made out.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
© svasti samastabhuvanāśraya śrīpṛthvīvallabha
kuḷatiḷakaṁ mallikāmōdaṁ jagadēkamalla śrīmajjaya
mācandrārkkatāraṁbaraṁ saluttamire śrīge nivāsamappa
gida vairibhūpa maleyāvanīpāḷakṛtāṁta nāga
ḍisikoṇḍu dōrjjayaśrīge nivāsamāgi jayasiṁghamahīśva
dhigatapaṁcamahāśabda mahāmaṇḍaḷēśvaraṁ banavāsipuravarēśvara
raprasāda sujanakaivāraṁ nārōhakatriṇētraṁ madadānedhavaḷa vyāḷaga
mātaṁgabhīmaṁ śaraṇāgatavajrapaṁjaraṁripukuṁjarāṁkuśaṁ Aribaḷatimiramūrttaṁṇḍanu
nte gaṇḍaṁ saṁgrāmarāmanabhimūnamēru vīravidyādharaṁ kaṭakada gōva subha ṭāridarppadaḷanaṁ mā
ṇḍalikalalāṭapaṭṭaṁ sattigana caṭṭaṁ śrīmadiṟivabeḍaṁgadēvara magaṁ śrīmatkuṁda rājaṁ banavāsipanni
rcchāsiramumanēkacchatracāyeyindamāḷuttamire . dēvagurubhakta kundaladēvana sati sava
tibennacammaṭi paṁpādēvi jiḍugūranāḷḍu yaśōvallari kūḍe parvvi sakaḷō vvareyoḷ . svastyana
varataparamakalyāṇābhyudayasahasraphaḷabhōgabhāgini dpitīyalakṣmīsamāneyara
ntaṟpuramukhamaṁḍaneyarkkundana manōnayanavallabheyarappa śrīmatpaṁpādēvi yar jiḍugū
ranāḷuttuṁ sukhasaṁkathāvinōdadinarasugeyyuttamire [kanda .] bāsavuranūṟanālvattu sakalakke
ydhe sēnamallara gōtrōdbhāsiya manneya kēsari manneyanenippinaṁ kaṇva nṛpaṁ . parirakṣi
sutire tannaṁ pa sōmēśvaradāsiyenipinaṁ biccara jiḍugūrai kabhōThe continuation is chipped off.
Digital edition of SII 18.52 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
